text: God remembered Noah and all the animals with him, and sent a wind over the earth, and the waters began to recede.
The springs of the deep and the floodgates of the sky were closed, and the rain stopped.
The water receded steadily, and after 150 days it had gone down.
On the 17th day of the seventh month, the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.
The waters kept receding until the tenth month, when the mountaintops became visible.
After 40 days Noah opened a window he had made in the ark.
He sent out a raven, which flew back and forth until the water dried up.
He also sent out a dove to see if the water had receded from the ground.
The dove found no place to land and returned to the ark, since water still covered the earth.
Noah waited seven more days and sent the dove out again.
It returned that evening with a freshly plucked olive leaf, showing the water had receded.
Noah waited seven more days and sent the dove out once more, and it did not return.
In Noah's 601st year, the water had dried up, and Noah saw the ground was dry when he removed the ark's covering.
By the 27th day of the second month, the earth was completely dry.
God told Noah to come out of the ark with his family.
He told him to bring out every living creature, so they could multiply on the earth.
Noah brought everyone and everything out of the ark.
Noah, his wife, his sons, and their wives came out.
Every animal and creature came out of the ark, kind by kind.
Noah built an altar to the LORD and offered burnt offerings of clean animals and birds.
The LORD was pleased and resolved never again to curse the ground or destroy all living things, despite human evil.
He promised that seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night would never cease.
